title: silene laciniata subsp. typica
intro: Silene laciniata subsp. typica, also known as California catchfly or fringed catchfly, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family, characterized by its pink or white, fringed flowers and deeply divided leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Silene laciniata subsp. typica include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the dried flowers and leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by using the plant's extracts in tinctures and salves.
